lines.text: The ancient Vampire Queen’s voice was ice-cold over the enchanted mirror.

lines.speaker: Queen Charlotte
lines.text: Lead the raid on Meadowbrook tonight. The court is starving.

lines.speaker: Queen Charlotte
lines.text: Bring back enough warm bodies to sate us… or you will be the one drained instead, my dear Lilith.
Lilith lowered her head, black lace frills trembling slightly.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: …Yes, Your Majesty. As you command.
The night air reeked of smoke and blood. Lesser vampires swarmed the once-peaceful village of Meadowbrook like locusts.
Screams echoed between burning cottages and trampled flower fields. Lilith watched from above, jaw tight, hating every second of it.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: (inner thought) I hate this…

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: But if I disobey Queen Charlotte… everyone under my command will suffer.
In the chaos, a young woman with flower ribbons in her braided hair stumbled out of her small bakery, eyes wide with terror.

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: (panicked, breathless) No… no no no! Mama? Papa?!

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: My home… everything is burning… This can’t be real!
She ran toward the cave, but it was too late. Two lesser vampires cornered her against an old stone wall, fangs bared, eyes glowing with hunger.

lines.speaker: Lesser Vampire 1
lines.text: Look at this soft one… so full of warm blood.

lines.speaker: Lesser Vampire 2
lines.text: She’s mine! I get the first taste!

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: Please… I don’t want to die…
Suddenly, a strong voice cuts through the night.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: HALT!
The lesser vampires froze instantly. Lilith descended gracefully, her goth-lolita dress fluttering in the night wind, crimson ribbons trailing behind her.
She stepped between her minions and the trembling human.
Elara pressed herself against the cold stone, tears streaking her cheeks, already accepting death.
Yet when Lilith approached, there was no violence in her steps — only intent.
Lilith gently tilted Elara’s chin upward with a gloved hand. Their eyes met.
Then Lilith leaned in slowly.
Her fangs sank into the soft skin of Elara’s neck with deliberate, sensual care — a deep, lingering bite.

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: Nngh…! Ahh…
A shaky moan escaped her as a strange warmth spread through her body.
Lilith’s free hand slid to Elara’s waist, pulling her closer.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: (soft, almost moaning)

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: …You’re different.
Elara’s knees weakened. Pain and unexpected pleasure twisted together.
When Lilith finally pulled back, a thin trail of blood ran down Elara’s collarbone.
Elara’s vision blurred. Her body went limp in Lilith’s arms as consciousness slipped away.
Elara slipped out of the lavish bedchamber and stepped into the vast, shadowy hallway of the vampire palace.

The corridor stretched endlessly in both directions, lit only by gigantic windows and its purple sky that cast long, dancing shadows on the stone walls. The air felt heavy and cold. Every footstep echoed far too loudly.

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: Oh no… Where am I? Where am I supposed to go…? Leaving the room was a terrible idea. If she finds out I’m trying to escape…
Her heart pounded in her chest. The hallway felt alive... As if the darkness itself could reach out and drag her into the void.
Suddenly, she heard the soft click of heels approaching from around the corner. Panicking, Elara quickly scanned her surroundings for a place to hide.
She spotted a dark, behind the tall velvet curtain in the alcove partially hidden behind a tall velvet curtain and a large decorative suit of armor.
It looked just deep enough to conceal her small frame.
Without thinking twice, she darted into the alcove and pressed herself against the cold stone wall, pulling the heavy curtain slightly in front of her.
Just in time.
The sound of footsteps grew louder. It was Lilith, which is the vampire who had bitten her.
She was walking slowly down the hallway while speaking in a low voice to another figure.
Elara held her breath inside the velvet alcove, heart hammering against her ribs.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: (irritated, voice sharp) Quiet, Heinrich! I am not in the mood today. Leave me alone.

lines.speaker: Heinrich
lines.text: But Madam Lilith, the village closest to Meadowbrook — Brookshaven — still has many healthy human citizens.

lines.speaker: Heinrich
lines.text: We could raid it tonight. Don’t you want to be promoted to General of the vampire legions?

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: I have no interest in that shit! I just want to be left alone in my room. Do not bother me anymore!

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: As you wish, Madam…
The sound of retreating footsteps echoed down the hall. Elara stayed perfectly still
Elara tried to move as quietly as possible, but her foot accidentally brushed against the edge of the armor stand.

lines.speaker: Heinrich
lines.text: Who’s there?! Bloody hell… a human?!

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: What the—?

lines.speaker: Heinrich
lines.text: Capture her—!
Before Heinrich could finish, Lilith moved like lightning. She grabbed the vampire and brutally bashed his head against the stone wall.
Heinrich slumped unconscious to the floor with a heavy thud.
Lilith whipped around, crimson eyes flashing with a dangerous mix of anger and something far softer.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: Goddamn it… I should have locked the door!

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: Miss Elara, don’t move. If the guards find you, they won’t be as gentle as I am. Just come with me and behave.

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: (frozen, stunned) Huh…?
Elara stood paralyzed, unable to speak or run. Her mind raced and wondering how does she know my name?
Lilith strode forward and firmly grabbed Elara’s wrist, pulling her back toward the bedchamber.
Lilith firmly guided Elara back onto the elegant four-poster bed draped in black lace and deep crimson silk.
She kept her expression cold and stern, trying to look every bit the intimidating Commander of the vampire fleet.
Elara’s heart sank as she stared up at the woman who had led the destruction of Meadowbrook.
Fear gripped her tightly once again.
Lilith locked the heavy bedroom door with a sharp click, then approached the bed.
She grabbed Elara’s chin with a gloved hand, tilting her face up.

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: Please… don’t kill me…

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: Stay quiet and don’t cause trouble. That’s all you need to do if you want to live.
Lilith leaned down and bit into Elara’s neck. Firm and efficient.
She drank quickly, taking what she needed without lingering.
Elara winced, enduring the sharp sting in silence.

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: Auugg!

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: Hmm, quiet please...

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: I... Think I will faint...

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: I apologize...
After a few moments, Lilith pulled back and licked the bite mark closed with a quick swipe of her tongue.
She released Elara’s chin and straightened up, keeping her distance.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: Don’t worry. I don’t plan to kill you yet.

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: …Why are you keeping me here?
Lilith paused for a moment, her usual commanding mask cracking slightly.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: If I wanted you dead, I would have already thrown you to the lesser vampires.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: he truth is… I had no choice but to attack Meadowbrook. Queen Charlotte gave the order directly. I couldn’t refuse.

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: So… destroying my village wasn’t your own decision?

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: I have no love for chaos or destruction. All I want is a quiet life… and maybe some strawberry cakes once in a while.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: Pathetic for a fleet commander, isn’t it?
She looked away, clearly uncomfortable showing even this much.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: But I’m supposed to be strong. I’m the commander of the fleet. I can’t appear weak in front of anyone.

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: So… you actually felt some pity for the people in Meadowbrook?

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: It was selfish to only save you… but I did it anyway.

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: I… understand. You were just following orders.
Lilith crossed her arms, regaining some of her composure.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: That’s exactly why you need to stay here for now. If Queen Charlotte finds out I’m hiding a human, it won’t end well for either of us.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: Tomorrow, I’ll take you to my witch friend. She can create something to keep you safe.

The corridor stretched endlessly in both directions, lit only by gigantic windows and its purple sky that cast long, dancing shadows on the stone walls. The air felt heavy and cold. Every footstep echoed far too loudly.

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: Oh no… Where am I? Where am I supposed to go…? Leaving the room was a terrible idea. If she finds out I’m trying to escape…
Her heart pounded in her chest. The hallway felt alive... As if the darkness itself could reach out and drag her into the void.
Suddenly, she heard the soft click of heels approaching from around the corner. Panicking, Elara quickly scanned her surroundings for a place to hide.
She spotted a dark, behind the tall velvet curtain in the alcove partially hidden behind a tall velvet curtain and a large decorative suit of armor.
It looked just deep enough to conceal her small frame.
Without thinking twice, she darted into the alcove and pressed herself against the cold stone wall, pulling the heavy curtain slightly in front of her.
Just in time.
The sound of footsteps grew louder. It was Lilith, which is the vampire who had bitten her.
She was walking slowly down the hallway while speaking in a low voice to another figure.
Elara held her breath inside the velvet alcove, heart hammering against her ribs.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: (irritated, voice sharp) Quiet, Heinrich! I am not in the mood today. Leave me alone.

lines.speaker: Heinrich
lines.text: But Madam Lilith, the village closest to Meadowbrook — Brookshaven — still has many healthy human citizens.

lines.speaker: Heinrich
lines.text: We could raid it tonight. Don’t you want to be promoted to General of the vampire legions?

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: I have no interest in that shit! I just want to be left alone in my room. Do not bother me anymore!

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: As you wish, Madam…
The sound of retreating footsteps echoed down the hall. Elara stayed perfectly still
Elara tried to move as quietly as possible, but her foot accidentally brushed against the edge of the armor stand.

lines.speaker: Heinrich
lines.text: Who’s there?! Bloody hell… a human?!

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: What the—?

lines.speaker: Heinrich
lines.text: Capture her—!
Before Heinrich could finish, Lilith moved like lightning. She grabbed the vampire and brutally bashed his head against the stone wall.
Heinrich slumped unconscious to the floor with a heavy thud.
Lilith whipped around, crimson eyes flashing with a dangerous mix of anger and something far softer.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: Goddamn it… I should have locked the door!
She stopped mid-sentence, eyes widening as she took in the full sight.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: (softer, almost breathless, a mix of irritation and longing) …Wait. You’re wearing my dress? How…?
Anger still simmered beneath the surface — at herself for being careless, at the situation, at how easily this human was unraveling her composure.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: It suits you far better than it ever did on me. Damn it… why do you have to look like that right now?
Lilith firmly guided Elara back onto the elegant four-poster bed draped in black lace and deep pink silk. She tried to look stern and intimidating,
She tried to look stern and intimidating, but her crimson eyes kept softening whenever they landed on the human.
Elara’s heart pounded as she stared up at the vampire commander who had led the attack on her village. Fear and confusion swirled inside her.
Lilith locked the heavy bedroom door with a loud click, then approached the bed.
She gently cupped Elara’s face with a gloved hand, tilting her chin upward.

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: Please… don’t kill me…

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: (softly) Shh… If you stay quiet and behave, nothing bad will happen to you.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: You took my dress, how dare you...

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: I apologize, your dress looks amazing I couldn't help myself but to wear it.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: Tsk, you have audacity to take my dress and look more cute, I have to take that from you now!

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: Don't run away cute girl, give me back my dress.
Lilith pins Elara down and gently removes her clothes.
Elara allows herself to stand bare before her, feeling vulnerable yet overwhelmed by an intense affection.
Elara slowly traces Lilith’s back, admiring her stunning vampire form as warmth stirs within her.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: Hmm... you have an incredible body.
Lilith couldn't hide the blush rising to her face at the sight of such a seductive figure. Elara may have been a simple baker girl, yet her beauty was undeniably alluring.
Lilith couldn't help herself as she moved closer and wrapped her arms around Elara from behind.
Lilith leaned down and sank her fangs slowly into Elara’s back with a deep, sensual bite
She drank quickly, taking what she needed without lingering.
As she drank, her free hand slid down over the her back and cupped Elara’s boobs, squeezing it gently but possessively.

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: (gasping, body tensing) Nngh…! Ahh…

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: I will make you cum, beautiful!

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: Miss Lilith... Keep going! Ahh ahh

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: Your pussy is incredibly wet now, goddamn!

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: Ahh ahhh Miss Lilith!!

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: I never feel so good like this! Ahh ahh!

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: Hmm! hmmm you are fucking delicious!

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: Ahh I think I will cum now aahhh!!
After a few moments, Lilith withdrew her fangs and licked the bite mark closed with slow, lingering strokes of her tongue.
She kept her hand on Elara’s chest a little longer, giving one final gentle squeeze before reluctantly pulling away.
After the incredible sex of the two girls, they began to clean themselves and wore their clothes.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: Ahh That was incredible! My lady Elara. There’s something special about you… something I can’t seem to resist.

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: That's okay, I was actually felt amazing after that experience. I never thought a vampire girl would take my virginity...
Lilith sat up straighter, looking a bit surprised at her own boldness.
For a moment, the tough commander’s mask slipped, revealing someone vulnerable and conflicted.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: You know what, I don’t fully understand it myself… but I feel this strong urge to keep you with me.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: I know I’m the reason Meadowbrook was destroyed… but I had no choice. It was a direct command from Queen Charlotte.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: I never wanted any of this chaos.

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: So… destroying my village wasn’t your real intention?

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: I have no interest in destruction or power. All I want is a quiet life… and to eat strawberry cakes and warm pumpkin pie.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: Strange for a vampire commander, isn’t it?
Lilith realized how much she was revealing to this human, yet she felt strangely comfortable. She kept talking...

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: But I’m supposed to act tough and strong... the reliable commander of the fleet. I can’t show weakness...

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: So… you actually felt pity for the people in Meadowbrook?

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: If I could have saved more of them, I would have.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: But defying Queen Charlotte would have been dangerous. It was selfish to only protect you, I know, but—

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: No… I understand.
Elara reached out and took Lilith’s gloved hand, giving it a comforting squeeze.
Elara reached out and took Lilith’s gloved hand, giving it a comforting squeeze.

lines.speaker: Elara
lines.text: You were just following orders. I’m glad you were still able to save someone like me. But if Queen Charlotte finds out about me—

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: (protective and firm) That’s exactly why you must stay here for now. You’re safe with me. Tomorrow, I’ll take you to my witch friend.

lines.speaker: Lilith
lines.text: She can create something that will keep you hidden and protected from the Queen’s eyes.
